name: Cherry Blossoms
recipeIngredient: 1 loaf (14-16 oz.) frozen white bread dough, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine confectioners' sugar and milk; mix well. Pour into a 9-inch deep-dish pie pan.
2. Sprinkle half of the cherries and pecans evenly over the sugar mixture.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll the bread dough into a 12x8 inch rectangle. Brush with melted butter.
4. In a small bowl, combine brown sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle over the dough.
5. Top with the remaining half of the cherries. Roll up the rectangle jelly roll style, starting from the long side. Pinch the edges to seal.
6. With a sharp knife, cut the roll into 12 slices.
7. Place the slices on top of the mixture in the pan. Let rise, covered, in a warm place for 30 minutes or until nearly doubled (or refrigerate wrapped in waxed paper and plastic wrap for 2-24 hours). Before baking, let chilled rolls stand, covered, for 20 minutes at room temperature.
8. Bake, uncovered, in a preheated 375°F oven for 20-25 minutes (25-30 minutes for chilled rolls) or until golden brown. If necessary, cover the rolls with foil during the last 10 minutes to prevent overbrowning. Let cool in the pan for 1-2 minutes. Invert onto a serving platter and serve warm. Makes 12 rolls.
